name: Fabulous Hot Chocolate
directions: In a saucepan, mix condensed milk and chocolate pieces.
Stir constantly over low heat until chocolate melts.
Cool to room Temperature.
Fold in the whipped cream.
Use now, or store covered in the fridge for up to one week.
To make the hot chocolate spoon a 1/4 cup of chocolate mix into a mug, add 1/2 cup of hot milk, stir and enjoy.

name: Homemade Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine the chocolate,sugar,cinnamon and a 1/2 cup of the milk in a small but heavy sauce pan.
Whisk constantly over a medium-low heat until the chocolate mixture melts and forms a smooth paste.
Add the remaining milk and whisk until warmed through.
Serve immediately.

name: Vanilla Milk
directions: Pour milk into a tall glass.
Add vanilla.
Add sugar.
Stir until completely mixed.
Enjoy.

name: Vanilla Milk
directions: Pour milk into cup or glass.
Add vanilla and sugar.
Stir well, add straw, enjoy!

name: Yoo Hoo Chocolate Drink
directions: Mix all of the ingredients in a blender for about 30 seconds  (Use a whisk if you don't have a blender).
Refrigerate until cold.

name: Cream  Soda Float
directions: Dissolve the sugar and the Kool-Aid in the hot water in a pitcher. Add the corn syrup and the vanilla extract. Sir well. Cover and chill syrup until cold. When the syrup is cold, pour 1/4 cup syrup into 1 cup cold club soda, stir gently and add 1 scoop of vanilla ice cream. (Make sure your cup is large enough because it will fizz and bubble!). Store remaining syrup in the refrigerator.

name: Oreo Sinful Shake
directions: Put all in a blender and mix.  You may have to turn it off and on and stirr it in between, but it is well worth it!

name: Tea Milk - Theemelk
directions: Heat milk to boiling point, taking care not to scorch; add tea bags and sugar to taste.
Steep for at least 3 minutes.
Serve hot or cold.

name: Mr. Steward's Favorite Hot Cocoa
directions: Place cocoa, sugar and salt in a saucepan.
Add 2/3 cup milk and heat while stirring to make a thick chocolate paste.
Add remainder of milk and vanilla.
Heat until hot.

name: Brown Cow
directions: Pour coca cola into a tall glass and add the cocoa powder. Stir until dissolved.
Add the ice cream. Stir quickly until foamy. Serve at once.

name: Low Fat Mexican Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine cocoa, Splenda & cinnamon in a jar, seal & shake to combine.
Whisk milk in a medium saucepan over low heat until hot, alternatively if you have a cappuccino maker, heat your milk using this.
To make: Place 2 tablespoons of choc mixture into 4 cups, pour over a little milk & stir to dissolve.
Add some milk froth to each cup & then top with remaining milk.
Add some marshmallows if you like & enjoy.

name: Chocolate Cream Soda
directions: In a tall glass, stir together half and half and syrup.
Stirring vigorously, add 2/3 c seltzer.  Serve immediately.

name: Fairy Milk
directions: Pour a glass of milk.
Stir in sugar until dissolved (adjust this amount according to the size of the glass).
Add a couple of drops of food coloring (or use more than one to create your own color).
Stir in vanilla.

name: Nutty Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine all ingredients in a large mug, mix well.

name: Banana Nut Hot Chocolate
directions: Combine all ingredients in a mug & mix well.

name: Vanilla Ice Cream Grape Soda
directions: Place the vanilla ice cream in a tall glass.
Fill the glasses up with the grape soda.
Stir if needed.

name: Hot Cocoa 1962
directions: Blend the cocoa powder, sugar, cold milk and cornstarch together well.
In a saucepot heat the one cup milk, to simmering point, and stir in the ingredients, and cook for one or two minutes, until dissolved and hot. Stirring well.
Pour into cup, top with mallows or whipped cream.

name: Honeycomb Hot Chocolate
directions: Warm 2 cups milk over low heat. When it starts to simmer, add a 50g chopped honeycomb bar and 70g chopped milk chocolate pieces. Stir constantly for 3-4 minutes or until chocolate is melted and well combined. Serve immediately with scoops of vanilla ice-cream, if desired.

name: Chocolate Grenada
directions: Mix dry ingredients together in a large saucepan. Gradually add milk, stirring to form a smooth paste.
When all milk has been added, heat chocolate mixture over medium heat, whisking constantly until almost boiling.
Remove pan from heat and stir in vanilla before serving.

name: It's Your Everyday Hot  Chocolate!
directions: Cook milk in a large  saucepan(non aluminum) over medium heat, stirring constantly, 6 to 8 minutes or until thoroughly heated (don't boil).
Whisk in chocolate syrup, fudge topping, and salt, whisking vigorously until chocolate is well blended and mixture is frothy.
Serve immediately with desired stir-ins.

name: Hot Dark Chocolate
directions: In 2-quart saucepan, heat milk to simmering over medium heat.
With wire whisk, stir in cocoa, sugar, chocolate, and vanilla. Heat mixture 3 minutes or until chocolate melts, stirring occasionally.
Pour into warm mugs to serve.
Variations:.
Mexican Hot Chocolate:
Prepare Hot Dark Chocolate as above, but add 1 cinnamon stick (3 inches long) to milk in step 1.
Coffee-Spiked Hot Chocolate:.
Prepare Hot Dark Chocolate as above, but stir 1/4 cup strong coffee(I use decaf)  into chocolate mixture just before serving.

name: (Hot Chocolate) Cocoa Blocks
directions: 1) Line an 8" x 8" pan with parchment paper or aluminum foil.
2) Heat the cream and condensed milk over low heat until steaming.
3) Remove from the heat and add the chocolate; allow it to gently melt.
4) After about 10 minutes, return the chocolate mixture to low heat to completely melt the chocolate.
5) Whisk vigorously until the mixture is thick and shiny.
6) Pour the chocolate mixture into the pan; shake the pan gently to level.
7) Set aside overnight to slowly set up.
8) Run a knife around the edge of the pan and turn out onto a clean cutting surface.
9) Slice into 1 ¼" cubes. Heat a knife in hot water and wipe dry before each cut, for smoothest cuts.
10) Stick a wooden stick into the center of each block.
11) Wrap in waxed paper, parchment, or plastic wrap to store.
12)  You can also add a few drops of flavoring oil (like hazelnut, coffee, or vanilla) to the mixture, or roll the blocks in cocoa or crushed candy canes, to kick it up a notch.

name: Rich Cocoa
directions: Heat the milk in a medium saucepan over medium-low heat, 4-6 minutes (do not let it boil).
To each mugs add 1/4 cup of the chocolate chips. Fill with the hot milk and stir until melted.
Tip: Instead of spoons, offer candy canes or cinnamon sticks for stirring.

name: Chocolate Chunk Hot Cocoa Mix
directions: In a bowl, combine the cocoa, sugar, and chocolate. Store in an airtight container. Keep at room temperature for up to 4 months.
Include these instructions with your gift: "In a small saucepan, whisk 1/4 cup cocoa mix with 3/4 cup milk. Bring to a bare simmer.

name: Bloody Hot Chocolate
directions: Bring milk and sugar to a simmer in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat, stirring until sugar dissolves, about 7 minutes.
Pour over chocolate, and let stand.
When chocolate begins to melt, stir until combined.
Whisk in food coloring.
Serve immediately with meringue bones.

name: Mums Better Than Mine Delicous Hot Chocolate
directions: put the kettle on the boil and put the hot chocolate inches.
then put in the sugar or sweetner.
mix it with the boiling water.
finally put the evaporated milk in stir the ingredients then leave to cool and sit back and enjoy.

name: Candy Cane Cocoa
directions: Heat milk until hot, but not boiling. Whisk in chocolate and crushed candy canes until melted.
Pour into 4 mugs; top with whipped cream and garnish each mug with a candy cane.

name: Homemade Protein Rich Cocoa
directions: If you want a nice thick, rich, creamy co-co -- play with the amt. of dry milk powder. I 'guesstimate' with it to get a consistancy I like.
I also mix 1/3 of the water in while warm (not hot) to blend it completely -- then add the really HOT stuff last!

name: Chocolate Almond Milk
directions: In a shaker bottle combine all the ingredients and shake until thoroughly combined. Serve and enjoy!

name: Eggnog Without the Egg
directions: In a punch bowl, combine the milk and pudding mix; mix well. Add the rum extract and 1/2 teaspoon nutmeg; mix well and set aside.
In a medium bowl, beat the heavy cream and confectioners' sugar until stiff peaks form. Add half of the whippped cream mixture to the milk mixture; stir until well blended.
Dollop the remaining whipped cream mixture over the top and sprinkle with the remaining 1/4 teaspoon nutmeg. Serve immediately.

name: Creamy Crockpot Hot Chocolate
directions: Low heat until chocolate chips are melted and everything is warm :).

name: Fire and Ice Hot Chocolate
directions: Heat milk in a sauce pan over medium high heat (any milk - skim, 2%, whole -- it all works just fine!).  Do NOT boil --  just heat thoroughly.  Wisk in vanilla and Nesquik chocolate powder.  I recommend 6 heaping spoonfuls of chocolate powder --  even more if you are a chocolate fan.  Don't be stingy --  it's chocolate!  Now put a scoop of your favorite vanilla ice cream in the bottom of a mug (about a 4th of a cup).  Poor your hot chocolate mixture over the ice cream.  Start drinking!  The creaminess of the ice cream makes the hot chocolate even more delicious.  You get a little cold with a little hot (fire and ice).  Less chance of the kids burning their tongue on the hot chocolate, too.  Bonus!  I hope you enjoy this yummy treat -- .

name: Starbucks Style Cold Coffee
directions: In lukewam water add coffee power and mix well. Save 4-5 pinch coffee powder. We'll use it later.
Blend it mixer.
Now add powdered sugar in mixer and blend again.
Add 1.5 Mug milk and again blend for minute.
Now add icecream an blend for 2 minute.
Keep the mixi jar in freezer for 10 minute. It will help get more foam.
After 10 Minutes pour in Mug and garnish with saved coffee powdered.
Serve Chilled with Chocolate biscuits, Muffins or cake.

name: Peppermint Hot Chocolate
directions: Mixed cocoa powder, sugar, salt, and 1/2 of the peppermint canes, in a small bowl.
You can warm the milk in either a microwave or on the stove. For the stove, pour milk into a small saucepan on medium-low heat. Be extra careful not to let it boil.
When the milk reaches the desired tempature mix milk and the mixture together.
If desired put a dollop of whipped cream on the beverage before sprinkling the last of the peppermint cane.
